key_insight: A stochastic, scale-aware contact model uses compounded Chapman–Kolmogorov equations to predict how rough-surface interfaces transition from elastic to elastic-plastic to fully plastic behavior under size-dependent hardness.
engineering_relevance: The framework helps engineers estimate contact pressure, elastic/plastic area fractions, and yield status for rough interfaces where surface roughness and material size effects strongly influence performance.
method: Formulate three integral equations for the scale evolution of elastic pressure, plastic contact area, and non-contact area.
Incorporate scale-dependent hardness into elastoplastic rough-contact statistics.
Use numerical solutions and curve fitting to identify elastic and plastic limits and build a rapid contact-status diagram.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical solutions, parameter sweeps, and curve-fitted limits that produce a new yield parameter and contact-status diagram, but it does not mention experimental validation.
watch_item: The model’s practicality may depend on accurate hardness scaling laws and reliable roughness statistics, which could be difficult to calibrate for real surfaces.
paper_title: A stochastic model for elastoplastic contact of rough surfaces incorporating scale-dependent hardness
